artifacts.notes: Approved as source-status only: the source itself states it is a guideline for responsible GenAI use within the Bilkent community.
Approved because the cited guideline directly states that GenAI use in all academic work is subject to academic integrity policy.
Approved as guidance-scoped; the source tells students to check syllabi and instructors for specific courses and assignments.
Approved because the cited recommendation uses mandatory language about not using GenAI unless the syllabus explicitly allows it.
Approved because the guideline directly addresses attribution for AI-generated content and academic misconduct risk.
Approved as privacy guidance; the claim is limited to the student-facing caution in the guideline.
Approved as teaching guidance; the claim uses recommended rather than mandatory wording.

artifacts.title: Bilkent University GenAI Guidance Source Review
artifacts.summary: Bilkent University has an official central GenAI guideline page for the Bilkent community. This staged bundle does not identify a broader standalone binding AI policy beyond that guideline; claims are limited to the guideline's source status, academic-integrity framing, course or syllabus permission model, attribution guidance, privacy caution, and faculty syllabus-statement recommendation.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Discovery found a central GenAI guideline, not a separate formal university-wide AI policy document.
Repository PDFs, department handbook material, and generic academic-integrity or quality-policy pages were not used for university-wide GenAI claims.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
